source.purpose: Raw Cloudflare dashboard defaults and available surfaces for a newly added zone. Menu labels are preserved as nested JSON keys so future UI captures can be compared by menu path.
source.maintenance: Update this capture when Cloudflare dashboard guidance changes, when the docs add or remove a setup recommendation, or when screenshots reveal renamed settings. Keep non-baseline surfaces in this data file when they help future reassessment, even if the public checklist only links to reference material.

Cloudflare product inventory.website_documentation: Keep the product inventory in the reference section, not as a blog post. Update this list when the baseline starts using or deliberately excludes another Cloudflare product or dashboard surface.

Managed robots.txt.description: When enabled, Cloudflare creates or updates robots.txt to signal that content should not be used for AI training.
Managed robots.txt.website_documentation: Document that this should remain off when the repository ships robots.txt and the repo should stay the source of truth.

Summary.empty_state: No requests from AI Crawlers with the selected filters

Configure Response.description: Set the response code and message shown to blocked crawlers.
Configure Response.website_documentation: Document the allowed paths when operators choose to block crawlers at Cloudflare. Keep robots.txt, llms.txt, llms-full.txt, and .well-known/* reachable; .well-known/* preserves access to security.txt.

Bulk block capture.confirmation: This action will set 15 crawlers (including Search Engine Crawlers) to Block.

Security.website_documentation: Document AI Crawl Control > Security as the crawler-specific blocking surface. Warn that blocking Search Engine Crawlers is intentionally aggressive and should match the operator's indexing posture.

Managed robots.txt.description: When enabled, Cloudflare creates or updates robots.txt to signal that content should not be used for AI training.

Agent Readiness.label: New
Agent Readiness.description: Check if your site is ready for AI agents by testing robots.txt and related configurations.

Robots.txt availability.website_documentation: Document that robots.txt should be reachable and owned by the repository; do not document request counts from the capture.

Robots.txt violations.empty_state: No violations found with the current filters.

Dashboards.website_documentation: Do not document the default dashboard cards as required configuration. Mention Cloudflare dashboards only in the analytics reference as optional infrastructure review surfaces.

Real User Measurements (RUM).message: No data available. Enable RUM for free to view the real user data.
Real User Measurements (RUM).website_documentation: Document in the analytics reference that vanityURLs does not require Cloudflare browser RUM and the baseline keeps it disabled.

Argo Smart Routing.website_documentation: Do not document as a default requirement for a redirector.

Workers.website_documentation: Document Workers analytics as the Cloudflare-side source for infrastructure request volume, errors, CPU and duration. Application-level redirects and misses belong to vanityURLs analytics.

Recommendations.message: Add an A, AAAA, or CNAME record for www and optionally create a redirect rule to send visitors to v8s.link.
Recommendations.label: New Alert
Recommendations.website_documentation: Document the www decision as optional. The apex Worker record is the required redirector path.

DNS management.website_documentation: Document the proxied Worker record for the apex short domain and keep mail policy records DNS-only.

DNS queries.website_documentation: Mention as an optional diagnostic surface for DNS propagation and unexpected query patterns. Do not document captured counts as defaults.

DNSSEC.status: Success! v8s.link is protected with DNSSEC.
DNSSEC.website_documentation: Document DNSSEC as recommended when the registrar delegation is correctly configured.

Multi-signer DNSSEC.website_documentation: Do not document as a default requirement.

Multi-provider DNS.website_documentation: Do not document as a default requirement.

Email Security.website_documentation: Do not document Cloudflare Email Security as a redirector requirement.

Email Routing.banner: New: Email Routing has a new home. Start using the new UI today.
Email Routing.website_documentation: Document email routing only when the operator wants Cloudflare to forward role addresses. It is not required for redirects.

DMARC Management.label: Beta
capabilities: Prevent email spoofing and email phishing
DMARC Management.website_documentation: Do not document Cloudflare DMARC Management as required; vanityURLs should document the DNS DMARC records instead.

capabilities: Fits into any email security stack
Email Security.website_documentation: Do not document Cloudflare Email Security as required for a redirector.

SSL/TLS encryption.website_documentation: Document Custom SSL/TLS with Full (Strict) as the desired production setting for vanityURLs. Be explicit that Automatic SSL/TLS can show a current applied mode such as Flexible before the operator selects Custom SSL/TLS.

Traffic Served Over TLS.website_documentation: Do not document captured traffic counts as defaults.

Manage Edge Certificates.website_documentation: Document that Universal SSL is enabled when an active Universal certificate appears here and the later action is Disable Universal SSL. Advanced Certificate Manager remains optional.

Advanced Certificate Manager.website_documentation: Do not document as a default requirement.

Total TLS.website_documentation: Do not document as a default requirement.

Cipher suites.website_documentation: Do not document as a default requirement.

Always Use HTTPS.website_documentation: Document On as a recommended production setting.

HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS).website_documentation: Document that Enable HSTS with max-age 0 is a non-enforcing or reset state, not production browser-enforced HSTS. Recommend a non-zero max age only after all public hostnames are HTTPS-ready, and includeSubDomains/preload only when the whole zone is intentionally HTTPS-only.

Minimum TLS Version.website_documentation: Document TLS 1.2 or stricter as the desired production setting.

Opportunistic Encryption.website_documentation: Do not document as a vanityURLs requirement.

Disable Universal SSL.website_documentation: Document as an action to avoid. Its presence means Universal SSL is currently enabled; the operator should not click it during normal setup.

Cloudflare-issued.website_documentation: Do not document client certificates or mTLS as default public redirector requirements.

Origin Certificates.website_documentation: Do not document origin certificates for a Worker-only redirector.

Authenticated Origin Pulls.website_documentation: Do not document unless an external origin is introduced.

Custom Hostnames.website_documentation: Do not document as a default requirement for a single operator-owned short domain.

Security Overview.description: Quickly identify security action items and view the security posture of your domain.

Security action items.title: Review and block AI bots from accessing your assets
Detect and mitigate automated traffic with Bot Fight Mode
Disrupt unwanted AI crawlers with AI Labyrinth
Configure your website's Security.txt to help security researchers

Traffic overview.website_documentation: Mention as an optional dashboard for edge security posture. Do not document captured traffic counts.

Analytics.description: Analyze incoming HTTP requests for your domain and learn how malicious traffic is mitigated by Cloudflare.

Request activity.website_documentation: Document as the place to review traffic blocked before the Worker.

Sampled logs.website_documentation: Mention sampled logs as a way to inspect probes such as robots.txt, abuse reports and scanner paths.

Web assets.description: Discover your web assets and instruct Cloudflare how to best protect them.

Endpoints.website_documentation: Do not document endpoint inventory as required for a redirector.

Discovery.website_documentation: Do not document web asset discovery as required for a redirector.

Schema validation.website_documentation: Do not document schema validation as required unless vanityURLs exposes explicit API schemas.

Security rules.description: Configure both Cloudflare managed rules and custom security rules.
controls: Search by id or rule name

Custom rules.website_documentation: Document custom WAF rules as recommended edge controls.

Rate limiting rules.website_documentation: Document rate limiting as an optional control for repeated misses or scanner-like traffic.

Managed rules.website_documentation: Document Cloudflare managed rules only when the plan supports them.

Settings.filter_usage_note: The dashboard exposes filter chips and search on this long page. The website checklist documents the settings in page order instead of using filters because filters hide context during setup review.

settings.name: AI Labyrinth
settings.website_documentation: Document Off for the default redirector because it modifies responses for bots. Treat as optional only.

settings.name: Block AI bots
configuration_options: Only block on hostnames with ads
settings.website_documentation: Document Block on all pages to block AI training crawlers across the zone without maintaining custom user-agent rules.

settings.name: Bot Fight mode
settings.configuration: On/off only on Free plan; no per-rule options
settings.website_documentation: Document On with the default configuration because there is no separate tuning UI.

settings.name: Browser integrity check
settings.website_documentation: Document On with the default configuration.

settings.name: Challenge passage
settings.website_documentation: Document 30 minutes as the recommended starting point.

settings.name: Client certificates
settings.website_documentation: Do not document as required.

settings.name: Cloudflare Managed Free Ruleset
settings.website_documentation: Document On as generic baseline managed coverage, not a vanityURLs-specific security posture.

settings.name: Continuous script monitoring
settings.website_documentation: Document Off for the default instance. The generated pages load one local script for UI niceness; enable only after adding third-party scripts or if the operator wants Cloudflare script inventory alerts.

settings.name: Custom fallthrough rules
settings.website_documentation: Document no default rule. Only needed when the operator deliberately wants fallback behavior for unmatched traffic.

settings.name: Email Address Obfuscation
settings.website_documentation: Document On. It is harmless without matching rules and useful when generated public pages expose role emails.

settings.name: Endpoint Labels
settings.website_documentation: Document no action. Endpoint labels are API Shield endpoint organization; vanityURLs does not expose an operator API.

settings.name: Hotlink Protection
settings.website_documentation: Document Off by default.

settings.name: HTTP DDoS attack protection
settings.website_documentation: Document as always active and Cloudflare-managed.

settings.name: IP access rules
settings.website_documentation: Do not document as required; prefer Cloudflare Access for private paths.

settings.name: IP lists
settings.website_documentation: Document only when custom rules need reusable IP sets.

settings.name: Leaked credentials detection
settings.website_documentation: Document Off unless the app has password login.

settings.name: Manage your robots.txt
configuration_options: Instruct AI bots to not scrape content
settings.website_documentation: Document Cloudflare-managed robots.txt disabled because the repository ships defaults/public/robots.txt and should remain the source of truth.

settings.name: mTLS rules
settings.website_documentation: Do not document as required.

settings.name: Network-layer DDoS attack protection
settings.website_documentation: Document as always active and Cloudflare-managed.

settings.name: Rate limit authentication requests
settings.website_documentation: Document no default rule. The redirector uses Cloudflare Access SSO for private paths and does not expose password authentication.

settings.name: Replace insecure JavaScript libraries
settings.current_scope_note: Primarily relevant to known third-party libraries such as polyfill
settings.website_documentation: Document On as low-risk future-proofing.

settings.name: Schema validation
settings.configuration: Default action: No action; no schemas configured
settings.website_documentation: Document no action unless explicit API schemas are configured.

settings.name: Security level
settings.website_documentation: Document disabled as the baseline. Use only for active incident response.

settings.name: Security.txt
settings.website_documentation: Document Configure before release.

settings.name: SSL/TLS DDoS attack protection
settings.website_documentation: Document as always active and Cloudflare-managed.

settings.name: User agent blocking
settings.website_documentation: Document only as optional; prefer precise custom WAF rules when possible.

settings.name: Web asset discovery
settings.website_documentation: Document no action. It can remain visible but does not change redirect behavior.

settings.name: Zone lockdown
settings.website_documentation: Document no action for the Free baseline. Cloudflare documents Zone Lockdown as unavailable on Free and recommends custom rules for allowlist-style behavior.

settings.name: New application security dashboard
settings.website_documentation: Document the current dashboard view through the navigation prose, not as an operator action.

Cloudflare Access.description: Put your applications behind a login screen.
Key capabilities: Require login via your identity provider before users can reach your app
Enforce multi-factor authentication and device posture checks
Protect self-hosted web apps, SaaS apps, private network resources and infrastructure
Grant access without a VPN or code changes
Cloudflare Access.WAF comparison: Access decides who gets to the application before WAF inspects allowed requests.
Cloudflare Access.website_documentation: Document Cloudflare Access as the primary control for private paths such as admin, tests, or operator-only pages.

Overview.description: Protect your applications and infrastructure with identity and device-based policies.
Recommendations: Connect and access a private web application
Connect your devices with the endpoint client
Add login methods for your applications

Recently modified applications.website_documentation: Do not document account-specific application names or user emails.

Access controls.website_documentation: Document only the Access application and policy concepts needed to protect vanityURLs paths.

Observatory.label: Beta
Observatory.RUM prompt: View core web vitals and network performance data by enabling Real User Monitoring (RUM).

Suggestions.title: Increase your cache hit ratio with Smart Shield

Observatory.website_documentation: Mention Observatory as an optional diagnostics surface. Do not require RUM for vanityURLs.

Origin Analytics.label: Beta
Origin Analytics.description: Monitor how the servers that host your content connect and respond to Cloudflare.

Top endpoints.empty_or_error_state: Failed to load data. Try again.

Origin Analytics.website_documentation: Do not document Origin Analytics as required for a Worker-only redirector.

Real User Measurements (RUM).message: No data available. Enable RUM for free to view the real user data.
Real User Measurements (RUM).website_documentation: Do not document Cloudflare RUM as required; vanityURLs uses server-side analytics for application events.

Recommendations.description: Initial server response time was short

Synthetic.website_documentation: Mention one-off synthetic tests as optional validation. Do not document captured scores or paid-product recommendations as defaults.

Measurement.name: Web analytics using real user measurement (RUM)

Site Recommendations.website_documentation: Document HTTP/2, HTTP/3, HTTP/2 to Origin and TLS 1.3 as useful defaults. Always Use HTTPS belongs with SSL/TLS production settings.

Smart Shield.website_documentation: Do not document Smart Shield as required for a Worker-only redirector.

Overview.description: Review your website or application cache performance.

Cache Reserve banner.message: Save money on reduced egress when you store assets in Cache Reserve.

Cache Analytics.website_documentation: Do not document paid Cache Analytics as required for vanityURLs.

Cache Reserve.website_documentation: Do not document Cache Reserve as required for a Worker-only redirector.

Purge Cache.website_documentation: Document purge only as a debugging tool; it does not replace Worker deployment or link inventory changes.

Caching Level.website_documentation: Document Standard as the baseline if Cloudflare cache settings are mentioned.

Browser Cache TTL.website_documentation: Document only for static assets, not redirect responses.

CSAM Scanning Tool.website_documentation: Do not document as a redirector requirement.

Always Online.website_documentation: Document Off by default; generated status pages should come from the Worker and repository.

Development Mode.website_documentation: Document Off except during active debugging.

Cache Rules.description: Specify which resources should be cached and for how long.
controls: Search by id or rule name

Cache Rules.baseline: No Cache Rules should exist for the redirector baseline.
Cache Rules.website_documentation: Document that Cache Rules are not part of the baseline. If any exist, disable or delete them before go-live.

Cache Response Rules.baseline: No Cache Response Rules should exist for the redirector baseline.
Cache Response Rules.website_documentation: Document that Cache Response Rules are not part of the baseline. If any exist, disable or delete them before go-live.

Tiered Cache.description: Reduce requests to customer origins by increasing cache hit ratios using Cloudflare's global network.
Tiered Cache.notice: Smart Tiered Cache is now offered as part of Cloudflare's origin server safeguard, Smart Shield.

Tiered Cache Topology.website_documentation: Do not document as required for a Worker-only redirector.

Cache Reserve.website_documentation: Do not document captured usage values or Cache Reserve as a default requirement.

Workers Routes.description: Map URL patterns to Workers, enabling Workers to be executed based on the route of an incoming request.
Workers Routes.default_state: You do not have any routes configured. Add a route to get started.
Workers Routes.website_documentation: Do not document zone-level Workers Routes as required when the instance uses a Worker custom domain for the apex short domain.

Overview.description: Modify HTTP requests and responses, perform URL redirects, configure Cloudflare settings and trigger actions for matching requests.

templates.name: Rewrite path for object storage bucket
Normalize encoded slash in URL path
Enable Email Obfuscation and Browser Integrity Check

Rule templates.website_documentation: Use modern Rules for edge controls when needed, but document only patterns vanityURLs operators should actually configure.

Snippets.website_documentation: Do not document Snippets as required.

Cloud Connector.label: Beta
Cloud Connector.description: Redirect traffic from your website or application to a defined storage bucket on Cloudflare R2 or supported third parties.
Cloud Connector.website_documentation: Do not document Cloud Connector as required for a Worker-only redirector.

Page Rules.description: Trigger certain actions whenever a request matches a defined URL pattern.
Page Rules.website_documentation: Do not document legacy Page Rules as the preferred setup path; use modern Rules or Worker behavior instead.

HTTP request headers.baseline: Keep off; Umami and Fathom do not need Cloudflare city, longitude, latitude, postal-code, or timezone headers.

HTTP response headers.baseline: Turn on as defense-in-depth; vanityURLs does not intentionally emit X-Powered-By.
Keep off by default; vanityURLs owns security headers in the Worker and defaults/public/_headers.

Managed Transforms.website_documentation: Document category rows for Managed Transforms. Recommend Remove X-Powered-By on as defense-in-depth. Keep visitor location headers off for privacy because analytics providers do not need precise Cloudflare location headers. Keep Add security headers off by default because the Worker and defaults/public/_headers own the app header policy.

captured_usage.scope: Lists are shared between all domains in the account.

Bulk Redirects.website_documentation: Document as an alternative for large static redirect lists, not a default Worker-based vanityURLs setup. Consider a blog or history note comparing vanityURLs v1 Page/Single Redirects, Bulk Redirects, and Worker registry behavior.

URL Normalization.website_documentation: Document incoming URL normalization as recommended.

Error Pages.label: New
Error Pages.description: Take full control of error responses with Custom Errors.
capabilities: Replace generic error pages with branded custom content
Serve different HTML, JSON, XML or plain text based on status codes or custom conditions
Write inline responses or fetch asset-based pages
Error Pages.website_documentation: Do not document Cloudflare Custom Errors as required; vanityURLs generated and custom status pages belong in the repository.

Network.description: Manage network settings for your website.

settings.name: IPv6 Compatibility
settings.website_documentation: Document On.

settings.name: gRPC
settings.website_documentation: Document Off.

settings.name: WebSockets
settings.website_documentation: Document Off unless a custom page requires WebSockets.

settings.name: Pseudo IPv4
settings.website_documentation: Document Off.

settings.name: IP Geolocation
settings.website_documentation: Document On because the Worker can use country metadata for aggregate analytics.

settings.name: Maximum Upload Size
settings.website_documentation: Document the lowest practical plan default.

settings.name: True-Client-IP Header
settings.website_documentation: Do not document as required.

settings.name: Network Error Logging
settings.website_documentation: Document On.

settings.name: Onion Routing
settings.website_documentation: Document On.

Web3 Gateways.description: Web3, or the distributed web, is a set of technologies that enables hosting content and web applications in a serverless manner by leveraging distributed systems and consensus protocols.
Web3 Gateways.website_documentation: Do not document Web3 Gateways as required for a vanityURLs redirector.

document: Keep Cloudflare Managed robots.txt off when the vanityURLs repository ships robots.txt.
Confirm /robots.txt returns 200 OK after deployment.
Use the proxied Worker DNS record for the apex short domain.
Keep mail policy DNS records DNS-only.
Use DNSSEC when the registrar delegation is configured correctly.
Use Full strict SSL/TLS mode, Universal SSL, TLS 1.3, Automatic HTTPS Rewrites and a minimum TLS version of 1.2 or stricter for production.
Enable HSTS only after every production hostname and subdomain is ready for HTTPS.
Use AI Crawl Control Security toggles only when the operator intentionally wants Cloudflare to block specific crawlers before the Worker.
Use Security Analytics and sampled logs for traffic blocked before the Worker.
Use custom security rules and rate limiting for scanner-like traffic and repeated short-link misses.

Keep Browser Integrity Check, Cloudflare managed ruleset, HTTP DDoS protection, Network-layer DDoS protection, SSL/TLS DDoS protection, Replace insecure JavaScript libraries and the new application security dashboard enabled.
Configure Security.txt before release.
Use Cloudflare Access for private vanityURLs paths such as admin, tests, or operator-only pages.
Use Speed Observatory and synthetic tests as optional diagnostics, not required setup.
Keep HTTP/2, HTTP/3, HTTP/2 to Origin and TLS 1.3 enabled when available.
Keep Cloudflare caching conservative: leave dynamic redirects to the Worker, keep Development Mode off except while debugging, and keep Cache Rules and Cache Response Rules empty.
Use the Worker custom domain for the apex short domain; zone-level Workers Routes are not required for the normal vanityURLs setup.
Use modern Rules sparingly for edge controls, prefer Worker and repository behavior for redirects, status pages and link inventory, and document URL normalization.
Keep Network settings simple: IPv6, IP Geolocation, Network Error Logging and Onion Routing on; gRPC and Pseudo IPv4 off; WebSockets off unless a custom page requires them.
Keep Cloudflare Web Analytics RUM disabled unless the operator intentionally wants browser-side performance telemetry outside the Worker event model.
do_not_document_as_required_setup: Zero request counts from the default capture.
Cloudflare Email Routing, DMARC Management and Email Security.
Advanced Certificate Manager, Total TLS, cipher suites, client certificates, origin certificates and custom hostnames.
DNS analytics captured counts and traffic breakdowns from the sample zone.
Captured TLS traffic counts from the sample zone.
Security Overview captured action-item dates and traffic counts.
Web assets endpoint inventory, discovery, schema validation and labels unless explicit API schemas are configured.
AI Labyrinth, client certificates, mTLS, Zone Lockdown, leaked credential controls and authentication rate limiting as default requirements.
Account-specific Access application names, user emails, login counts and connected-user counts.
Cloudflare RUM, Origin Analytics, Smart Shield, Speed Brain, Polish, Argo, Zaraz and synthetic test scores as default requirements.
Cache Analytics, Cache Reserve, Crawler Hints, Always Online, Query String Sort, Tiered Cache and captured cache usage as default requirements.
Zone-level Workers Routes as a default requirement when the Worker custom domain is active.
Rules templates, Snippets, Cloud Connector, legacy Page Rules, Bulk Redirects and Cloudflare Custom Errors as default requirements.
True-Client-IP and visitor IP/location managed transforms as default requirements.
Per-crawler activity counts from an idle zone.
Web3 Gateways as a default requirement.
